heading: Notification of significant foreign cyber intrusions and active measures campaigns directed at elections for Federal offices
label: 3371c Notification of significant foreign cyber intrusions and active measures campaigns directed at elections for Federal offices
notes: DefinitionsFor definition of “congressional intelligence committees” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 5003 of div. E of Pub. L. 116–92section 3003 of this title

children.heading: Active measures campaign
children.label: 1 Active measures campaign
children.text: The term “active measures campaign” means a foreign semi-covert or covert intelligence operation.

children.heading: Candidate, election, and political party
children.label: 2 Candidate, election, and political party
children.text: The terms “candidate”, “election”, and “political party” have the meanings given those terms in .section 30101 of title 52

children.label: A
children.text: The majority leader of the Senate.

children.label: B
children.text: The minority leader of the Senate.

children.label: C
children.text: The Speaker of the House of Representatives.

children.label: D
children.text: The minority leader of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Cyber intrusion
children.label: 4 Cyber intrusion
children.text: The term “cyber intrusion” means an electronic occurrence that actually or imminently jeopardizes, without lawful authority, electronic election infrastructure, or the integrity, confidentiality, or availability of information within such infrastructure.

children.label: A
children.text: The Federal Government.

children.label: B
children.text: A State or local government.

children.label: C
children.text: A political party.

children.label: D
children.text: The election campaign of a candidate.

children.heading: Federal office
children.label: 6 Federal office
children.text: The term “Federal office” has the meaning given that term in .section 30101 of title 52

children.heading: High confidence
children.label: 7 High confidence
children.text: The term “high confidence”, with respect to a determination, means that the determination is based on high-quality information from multiple sources.

children.heading: Moderate confidence
children.label: 8 Moderate confidence
children.text: The term “moderate confidence”, with respect to a determination, means that a determination is credibly sourced and plausible but not of sufficient quality or corroborated sufficiently to warrant a higher level of confidence.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Foreign Relations, the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs, and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Foreign Affairs, the Committee on Homeland Security, and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Determinations of significant foreign cyber intrusions and active measures campaigns
children.label: b Determinations of significant foreign cyber intrusions and active measures campaigns

children.text: that on or after , a significant foreign cyber intrusion or active measures campaign intended to influence an upcoming election for any Federal office has occurred or is occurring; andDecember 20, 2019
with moderate or high confidence, that such intrusion or campaign can be attributed to a foreign state or to a foreign nonstate person, group, or other entity.

children.label: A
children.text: A description of the significant foreign cyber intrusion or active measures campaign, as the case may be, covered by the determination.

children.label: B
children.text: An identification of the foreign state or foreign nonstate person, group, or other entity, to which such intrusion or campaign has been attributed.

children.label: C
children.text: The desirability and feasibility of the public release of information about the cyber intrusion or active measures campaign.

children.label: D
children.text: Any other information such Directors and the Secretary jointly determine appropriate.

children.heading: Electronic election infrastructure briefings
children.label: 2 Electronic election infrastructure briefings
children.text: With respect to a significant foreign cyber intrusion covered by a determination under subsection (b), the Secretary of Homeland Security, in consultation with the Director of National Intelligence and the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, shall offer to the owner or operator of any electronic election infrastructure directly affected by such intrusion, a briefing on such intrusion, including steps that may be taken to mitigate such intrusion. Such briefing may be classified and made available only to individuals with appropriate security clearances.

children.heading: Protection of sources and methods
children.label: 3 Protection of sources and methods
children.text: This subsection shall be carried out in a manner that is consistent with the protection of sources and methods.
